name: opc-develop
description: Product development suite from fastest human-accepted vibe coding to taste-first design, contract TDD, evidence-labeled verification, retro, and executable harness capabilities

interface.shortDescription: Fast vibe coding through contract TDD, verification, and retro
interface.longDescription: opc-develop (OPC = One Person Company) is a loop-engineering development workflow suite for Claude Code and Codex. The full flow captures the builder's taste in three artifacts — a grilled decision-first requirement (brainstorm), an experienceable prototype inside the real codebase (demo), and a gated PRD with numbered acceptance criteria (prd, ending in a pushed product-to-architecture handoff), and a technical design with ADR-style decision records after an architecture intake pass (architect) — then hands execution to agents: a build skill that completes everything local in one run — contract partitioning, TDD implementation with RED/GREEN evidence, mock retirement, local deploy with dev-environment migrations, and full black-box regression (build), a test-environment release with gated manifest, automated regression, and the human acceptance touchpoint (ship), a fail-closed production release with preflight, backups, prod-safe regression, and a watch window (deploy), and production incident response with diagnostic reports and expedited hotfixes (oncall). Lite handles small changes with zero ceremony. Retro mines per-feature ledgers and the error ledger for rework routing and recurring mistakes, crystallizing human-approved rules at the lowest enforcement layer. Harness assesses and builds the project's run/reset/observe/drive capabilities by executing them. Reviews are gated by content-SHA freshness and structural validation scripts. Output and artifact language must follow the applicable project AGENTS.md target language; only machine-readable tokens stay normative. Where isolated subagents are unavailable, gates degrade honestly with recorded labels instead of blocking.
defaultPrompt: Use $vibe to implement this immediately without tests; I will perform the acceptance.
Use $lite to handle this small request on the current branch with targeted tests and before/after evidence.
Use $brainstorm to grill this raw idea into a decision-first requirement with a numbered feature branch.
Use $retro to audit measured cost and data quality, replay compressed incident cases, verify rule effectiveness, and present a plain-language HTML report.
